##NAMES=Tomcampbellite
##RRUFFID=R240012
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[KCl][Fe^2+^_14_(OH)_6_(PO_4_)_6_(PO_3_OH)_2_]
##LOCALITY=Dan Patch mine, near Keystone, Pennington County, South Dakota, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Tom Loomis
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless, transparent feather-like crystals lying flat on cleavage planes of triphylite.  Associated on the sample with childrenite, hureaulite, kryzhanovskite, ludlamite, and reddingite. Type sample
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R240012
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[K_0.98_Cl_0.98_][(Fe_11.28_Mn_2.03_Ca_0.60_Mg_0.09_)(OH)_6_(P_1.00_O_4_)_6_(P_1.00_O_3_OH)_2_]
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 16.478 b: 6.296 c: 16.728 alpha: 90 beta: 115.46 gamma: 90 volume: 1567.1 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
